data.title: As a practitioner, story validation failure creates remediation tasks via TDD
data.description:
## Description

**What**: When all tasks for a story are closed but story-level validation fails, sprint invokes implementation-plan to create additional remediation tasks following standard TDD flow (RED test first, then implementation).
**Why**: Today, a story that passes all individual tasks but fails validation is a dead end. The practitioner must manually create remediation tasks. Automated remediation keeps the sprint loop intact.
**Scope**:
- IN: Story validation failure detection (all tasks closed, validation fails), implementation-plan invocation for remediation, TDD task pair creation (RED first), REPLAN_TRIGGER: validation comment
- OUT: Epic-level validation (Story 5), brainstorm escalation

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, sprint detects when all tasks for a story are closed but story-level validation fails, and invokes implementation-plan to create remediation tasks
  ← Satisfies: "When all tasks for a story are closed but story-level validation fails, sprint invokes implementation-plan"
- When this story is complete, remediation tasks follow standard TDD flow with RED test task preceding implementation task
  ← Satisfies: "create additional remediation tasks following standard TDD flow"
- When this story is complete, a REPLAN_TRIGGER: validation comment is recorded on the epic
  ← Satisfies: "each trigger records a structured REPLAN_TRIGGER"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Testing] Needs integration test: mock story with closed tasks + failing validation → verify remediation tasks created

## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
